menu_title: Going Over Their Head -- Escalating Without Making an Enemy
menu_desc: The last resort: what you tried, the cost, and what you need from your boss
ch3_sub: You did everything right, and she still has not moved
ch5_sub: Your boss will test you before she helps you
ch6_sub: Quick fire, three role-plays, and a boss who asks what you have tried

plan_row.tema: Going Over Their Head -- Escalating Without Making an Enemy
plan_row.foco: Present perfect for attempts (I have asked twice) + I would like you to + verb + the cost with a date
plan_row.atividade: Escalation note + role-play with a manager who tests you before helping
plan_row.homework: Record a 2-min escalation: what you tried, the cost, the specific ask

heading: When the Nudge Does Not
Work
warmup.intro: In lesson 15 you nudged Camila, and she promised you this:

callback.text: Could you get it to me by Thursday?
callback.speak: Could you get it to me by Thursday?

warmup.box_title: Thursday came. Nothing came with it.
warmup.box_text: You have asked three times. You offered help. The sprint starts on Monday, and you still have no document. Now you have to go to her director -- without making an enemy.

goal.d: Three role-plays, and my first answer is no

heading: The Language of the Last
Resort
vocab_ch.sub: 8 words to go above someone, and still work with them tomorrow

vocab.def: The final option, when everything else has failed
vocab.def_pc: the final option, when everything else has failed
vocab.hint: The final option, after everything failed
vocab.ex: Escalation is a last resort, not a first move.

vocab.def: To bring another person into the problem
vocab.def_pc: to bring another person into the problem
vocab.ex: I have to involve her director now.

vocab.def: To support you publicly, in front of others
vocab.def_pc: to support you publicly, in front of others
vocab.hint: To support you in front of others
vocab.ex: I need you to back me up in the meeting.

vocab.def: Open: you tell people what you are doing
vocab.def_pc: open: you tell people what you are doing
vocab.ex: I was transparent: I told her I would escalate.

vocab.def: The bad feeling left between two people
vocab.def_pc: the bad feeling left between two people
vocab.hint: The bad feeling left between two people
vocab.ex: There is tension now, and I have to manage it.

vocab.def: To say that something is true, even when it hurts you
vocab.def_pc: to say that something is true, even when it hurts you
vocab.hint: To say it is true, even when it hurts
vocab.ex: I admit I should have asked her earlier.

vocab.def: To end a problem, for good
vocab.def_pc: to end a problem, for good
vocab.hint: To end the problem for good
vocab.ex: I want to resolve this today, not to win it.

vocab.def: Official: written down, on the record
vocab.def_pc: official: written down, on the record
vocab.ex: This is not a formal complaint about her.

heading: The Time You Stayed
Silent
vocab_check.prompt: Think of a time at Oracle when you should have escalated, and you waited too long.
vocab_check.sub: Talk for 90 seconds: who was blocking you, why did you not escalate, and what did the silence cost?

heading: How to Escalate Without Burning the
Bridge
listenings.text: So you want me to call her director. Fine. But before I do, understand what you are asking for. The moment I call him, Camila will hear about it in ten minutes, and you will work with her for the next two years. So do it properly. First, tell me exactly what you have tried, with dates -- three attempts, an offer of help, a deadline she agreed to. Second, tell me the cost of not solving it, with a number and a date. Third, tell me exactly what you want me to do. Not "help me" -- what, precisely? And Diogo, one rule: you tell her, today, before I call anybody. If she hears it from her director first, you have made an enemy for life. Escalation is a last resort, and it is never a surprise.
comp: What three things does Sophia want before she calls?
What he has tried with dates, the cost with a number and a date, and exactly what he wants her to do.
That Diogo tells Camila first, today, before anybody calls her director.
What happens if Camila hears it from her director first?
Diogo makes an enemy for life.

heading: The Escalation,
Done Well
listenings.lead: This is the target. Listen, then do it your way.
listenings.text: Sophia, I need to involve you, and this is a last resort. Here is what I have tried. I asked Camila on the fifth, in writing. I nudged her on the ninth, and I made the ask smaller -- one page instead of the full document. On the twelfth she agreed to send it by Thursday, and I offered to send someone from my team to help her write it. Thursday came, and nothing came with it. I have had no answer since. The cost: my team cannot start the tests, the sprint starts Monday, and every day of waiting is a day on the go-live. Here is what I would like you to do: one call to her director, today, asking for the endpoint list by Wednesday. Not a complaint about her -- a request for the page. And I want to be transparent with you: I am telling Camila this afternoon that I am escalating, before you call anybody. I want the document. I do not want a war.
comp: What are the three attempts he lists?
He asked on the fifth, nudged on the ninth with a smaller ask, and on the twelfth she agreed to Thursday.
What exactly does he want Sophia to do?
One call to Camila's director, today, asking for the endpoint list by Wednesday.
What will he do before Sophia calls?
He will tell Camila this afternoon that he is escalating.

heading: The Escalation
Note
artefact.title: Three Attempts. One Ask.
artefact.reason: What I need: the endpoint list -- one page -- from the integration team
rows: Tried: asked on the 5th (in writing), nudged on the 9th (smaller ask), agreed date on the 12th
Offered: someone from my team to write it with her. No answer since Thursday
Cost: the sprint starts Monday; every day of waiting is a day on the go-live
Ask: one call to her director today -- the page by Wednesday. And I tell Camila first.
artefact.footer: Not a complaint about a person. A request for a page. And never, ever a surprise.

grammar.prompt: Which one proves you tried? Which one shows the cost? Which one asks for something real?

discovery.html: "I have asked her three times, and I have offered help."
discovery.speak: I have asked her three times, and I have offered help.

discovery.html: "I have been waiting for two weeks."
discovery.speak: I have been waiting for two weeks.

discovery.html: "If we do not have it by Wednesday, we lose the sprint."
discovery.speak: If we do not have it by Wednesday, we lose the sprint.

discovery.html: "I would like you to call her director today."
discovery.speak: I would like you to call her director today.

rows: I have + past participle (asked, nudged, offered) + times / dates
If we do not + verb + by + day, we + result
If we do not have it by Wednesday, we lose the sprint.
I would like you to + verb (never just "help me")
I would like you to call her director today.
grammar.note: Escalation is a last resort, and it is never a surprise. You escalate the PROBLEM, not the person -- and you tell the person first, today, before anybody calls anybody. Tomorrow you still have to work with her.
mistakes: I am waiting since two weeks.
I have been waiting for two weeks.
She did not answered my emails.
She did not answer my emails.
grammar.mistake_note: Tempo que começou no passado e continua: I have been waiting FOR two weeks (nunca "I am waiting since"). E depois de did not vem o verbo no infinitivo: did not answer, nunca "did not answered".

heading: How to Escalate Without Making an
Enemy
bank.sub: What you tried → what you offered → the cost, with a date → the precise ask → the person hears it from YOU
bank.label: The five lines of an escalation
items: I need to involve you, and this is a last resort.
I have asked three times, and I offered someone from my team to help.
The sprint starts Monday, and every day of waiting is a day on the go-live.
I would like you to call her director today, and ask for the page by Wednesday.
This is not a complaint about her -- I want the document, not a war.
I am telling Camila this afternoon, before anybody calls anybody.

scenarios: A vendor has missed three deadlines. Their account manager answers nothing. Your go-live is in three weeks.
A director from another area keeps taking two developers off your project, without telling you.
The client's security team has not answered for a month. Your boss asks: why is this my problem?
scenarios_sub: Sixty seconds each: what you tried, the cost with a date, the precise ask -- and who you tell first.

heading: What Have You
Tried?

lines.html: Sophia, I need to involve you. This is a last resort -- the integration team is blocking my sprint.
lines.speak: Sophia, I need to involve you. This is a last resort. The integration team is blocking my sprint.

lines.html: Before I do anything: what have you tried?
lines.speak: Before I do anything: what have you tried?

lines.html: I have asked three times -- the fifth, the ninth, the twelfth. I made the ask smaller, and I offered someone from my team to help write it.
lines.speak: I have asked three times: the fifth, the ninth, the twelfth. I made the ask smaller, and I offered someone from my team to help write it.

lines.html: And the cost? Give me a number, not a feeling.
lines.speak: And the cost? Give me a number, not a feeling.

lines.html: The sprint starts Monday. If we do not have the page by Wednesday, we lose a week on the go-live.
lines.speak: The sprint starts Monday. If we do not have the page by Wednesday, we lose a week on the go-live.

lines.html: What do you want from me? And do not say "help".
lines.speak: What do you want from me? And do not say help.

lines.html: One call to her director today. Ask for the endpoint list by Wednesday. Not a formal complaint about her -- a request for a page.
lines.speak: One call to her director today. Ask for the endpoint list by Wednesday. Not a formal complaint about her, a request for a page.

lines.html: She will hear about it in ten minutes. There will be tension. Have you thought about that?
lines.speak: She will hear about it in ten minutes. There will be tension. Have you thought about that?

lines.html: Yes. I am telling her this afternoon, before you call. I want to be transparent -- and I admit I should have escalated a week ago.
lines.speak: Yes. I am telling her this afternoon, before you call. I want to be transparent, and I admit I should have escalated a week ago.

comp: What is the first thing Sophia asks?
What does she refuse to accept as an ask?
What does Diogo admit at the end?
That he should have escalated a week earlier.

tips: 'I need to involve you, and this is a last resort.'

quickfire.situation: Prove you tried -- three attempts, with dates.
tips: 'I have asked three times: the fifth, the ninth, the twelfth.'

quickfire.situation: Your boss says: 'Why is this my problem?' Answer.
tips: Give the cost, with a date.
Show what only she can unlock.
Say what, to whom, and by when.

quickfire.situation: She asks: 'And what about the relationship?' Answer.
tips: 'I am telling her before anybody calls.'
You escalate the problem, not the person.

quickfire.situation: Now tell Camila you are escalating -- to her face.
tips: Say it before it happens, not after.
'I still want the page, not a war.'

items: Why did you wait three weeks to tell me this?
Are you asking me to fight with another director because of one page?
If I make this call, how do you work with her next month?
pressure.followup: Attempts first, cost second, precise ask third. And the person always hears it from you -- before anybody else.

heading: Ask for the
Call
guided.prompt: Ten minutes with Sophia. Get her to call the director.
guided.sub: Use the escalation note. Five moves, in this order:
chips: I would like you to call her director

heading: Her First Answer Is
No
semi.quote: Sort it out yourself. I am not calling another director over one page.
semi.sub: She refuses twice: first she tells you to handle it, then she asks what it costs the relationship. Do not get emotional and do not blame Camila. Prove the attempts, give the number, and make the ask so small that saying no becomes expensive.
chips: I have tried everything I can
three attempts, and an offer of help
if we do not have it by Wednesday...

heading: Your Real
Escalation
free.headline: Two minutes. No notes. One precise ask.
free.sub: Take a real blocker at Oracle that has not moved in weeks. Escalate it out loud: what you tried, with dates, what you offered, the cost with a date, the precise ask -- and say what you will tell the person first.

survival.en: I need to involve you, and this is a last resort.
survival.pt: Preciso te envolver, e isso é último recurso.

survival.en: I have asked three times, and I offered help.
survival.pt: Já pedi três vezes, e ofereci ajuda.

survival.en: If we do not have it by Wednesday, we lose the sprint.
survival.pt: Se não tivermos até quarta, perdemos a sprint.

survival.en: I would like you to call her director today.
survival.pt: Eu gostaria que você ligasse para o diretor dela hoje.

survival.en: I am telling her before anybody calls anybody.
survival.pt: Vou avisar a ela antes de qualquer telefonema.

checks: I escalate only as a last resort, and I can say why.
I can prove what I tried: three attempts, with dates.
I give the cost with a number and a date.
I ask for something precise -- never just "help".
I tell the person first. No surprises, no enemies.

closing.line: You went over her head, and you can still work with her on Monday.
closing.next_desc: Defending your project in front of a committee that is looking for a mistake

order_audio.text: Diogo tells Sophia he needs to involve her, as a last resort. She asks what he has tried, and he lists three attempts, with dates. She asks for the cost, and he gives it: the sprint starts Monday. Then he makes a precise ask: one call to Camila's director today. Finally, he says he will tell Camila himself, this afternoon, before anybody calls.
items: She asks for the cost, and he gives it: the sprint starts Monday.
Diogo says he needs to involve Sophia, as a last resort.
He says he will tell Camila himself, before anybody calls.
Sophia asks what he has tried, and he lists three attempts with dates.
He makes a precise ask: one call to her director today.

preclass.desc: Escalate without making an enemy: what you tried, the cost with a date, the precise ask, and the person hears it from you.
preclass.context: Three attempts and no answer. Escalation is a last resort, and Diogo has reached it. He goes to Sophia: "I need to involve you." She tests him before she helps: what have you tried? "I have asked three times -- the fifth, the ninth, the twelfth. I made the ask smaller, and I offered someone from my team to help." Then the cost, with a date: if the page does not arrive by Wednesday, the sprint is lost. Then the ask -- and it is precise, never "help me": "I would like you to call her director today." This is not a formal complaint about a person; it is a request for a page. Diogo knows there will be tension, so he is transparent: he tells Camila himself, this afternoon, before anybody calls anybody. And he admits his own mistake: he should have escalated a week ago. He wants to resolve the problem -- not to win a war -- because on Monday he still needs her to back him up.
quiz_ctx: Why does Diogo tell Camila before Sophia calls?
Because an escalation must never be a surprise -- or he makes an enemy for life.
Because he wants her to send the page in the next hour.
Because Sophia asked him to keep it secret.
I have been waiting for two weeks.
I am waiting since two weeks.
What is a good ask when you escalate?
"I would like you to call her director today."
preclass.tip_sub: Escalar sem virar inimigo: provar o que tentou, mostrar o custo e pedir algo preciso. / Escalating without making an enemy.
tip_rows: If we do not + verbo + by + dia, we + resultado
If we do not have it by Wednesday, we lose the sprint.
I would like you to + verbo (nunca só "help me")
I would like you to call her director today.
preclass.tip_key: Duração que continua: I have been waiting FOR two weeks -- nunca "I am waiting since two weeks". Depois de did not, verbo puro: did not answer. E a regra que salva a relação: a pessoa ouve de VOCÊ, antes de qualquer telefonema.

blanks.hint: Hint: the final option, after everything failed / último recurso
blanks.phrase: I need to involve you, and this is a last resort.

blanks.hint: Hint: present perfect: ___ her three times (asked, and it still matters now)
blanks.phrase: I have asked her three times, and I have offered help.
blanks.post: her three times, and I have offered help."

blanks.hint: Hint: ___ two weeks (duration) -- nunca "since two weeks"
blanks.phrase: I have been waiting for two weeks.

blanks.hint: Hint: I would like you ___ call her director (particle before the verb)
blanks.phrase: I would like you to call her director today.

blanks.hint: Hint: open -- you tell people what you are doing / transparente
blanks.phrase: I want to be transparent: I am telling her today.

blanks.hint: Hint: to say it is true, even when it hurts / admitir
blanks.phrase: I admit I should have escalated a week ago.
blanks.post: I should have escalated a week ago."

preclass.speech_intro: The five lines of a clean escalation. Record each one. Calm and factual -- anger in your voice turns a request into a complaint.
quiz_sit: A peer has blocked you for three weeks, after three attempts. You:
Go to your manager with what you tried, the cost, and a precise ask -- and tell the peer first.
Send an angry email with your manager in copy.
Wait another week and hope it moves.
Your manager asks: "What do you want from me?" The best answer is:
"One call to her director today, asking for the page by Wednesday."
"I want you to help me with this."
"I want you to talk to her about her attitude."
Before your manager calls the other director, you:
Tell your colleague yourself, today, that you are escalating.
Say nothing -- it is not your problem anymore.
Ask your manager to keep your name out of it.
think: Take a real blocker at Oracle that has not moved in weeks. Escalate it out loud, for two minutes: (1) this is a last resort, (2) what you tried -- three attempts, with dates, (3) what you offered, (4) the cost, with a number and a date, (5) the precise ask. Record it.

Now your manager says: "Sort it out yourself. I am not calling another director over one page." Answer out loud. Do not blame the person, do not get emotional -- prove the attempts, give the number, and make the ask smaller. Record it.

complementary.title: Margin Call (2011)
complementary.desc: A junior analyst finds a big problem. Watch how the news goes up level by level, all the way to the CEO, in one night.
complementary.tip: Watch one escalation scene and note how each person explains the problem to the level above.

complementary.title: Skills 360 -- Managing Up 2: How to Do It
complementary.desc: How to work well with your boss, and how to bring problems to him or her in the right way.
complementary.tip: Take notes and plan how you would ask your manager for help with a blocked peer.

complementary.title: How to Escalate Issues as a Project Manager
complementary.desc: When to escalate, when to wait, and how to do it without making enemies.
complementary.tip: Write the escalation message you would send, using the structure from the video.

teacher.title: Abertura (2 min): Compartilhe a tela. Diga: 'In lesson 15 you nudged her, twice, and you were perfect. It did not work. Today you go over her head -- and you still have to work with her next year.' Avise: hoje ele fala quase o tempo todo.
teacher.warmup: Warm-up falado (4 min): Toque a frase da Aula 15. Depois a virada: 'Thursday came. Nothing came with it.' Faca a pergunta do box e cale a boca 90 segundos.
teacher.goal: Objetivo (1 min): 'Words, one move, and then you escalate to me three times -- and my first answer is no.' Rapido.
teacher.vocab_transition: Transicao vocab (1 min): 'Eight words. Click each card.' Ferramenta, nao aula de vocabulario.
teacher.vocab1: Vocab reveal 1-4 (4 min): Pista, tentativa, reveal, audio, uma frase real. CCQ 'last resort': 'Is escalation a first move? (No -- the last one.)' CCQ 'transparent': 'If I escalate and say nothing to the person, am I transparent? (No.)' CCQ 'back me up': 'In private or in front of others? (In front of others.)'
teacher.vocab2: Vocab reveal 5-8 (4 min): Mesma dinamica. CCQ 'tension': 'Does escalation create tension? (Yes -- always. You manage it, you do not avoid it.)' CCQ 'admit': 'Is admitting a mistake weak? (No -- it buys credibility.)' CCQ 'formal': 'Is a chat message formal? (No.)'
teacher.vocab_check: Vocab check falado (4 min): 90 segundos sobre uma vez em que ele deveria ter escalado e esperou demais. Minimo cinco palavras novas. Voce so escuta e conta.
teacher.ch3: Transicao (1 min): 'You did everything right. She still did not move.' Deixe a frustracao aparecer -- e dela que sai a fala.
teacher.listening1: Listening 1 (4 min): 'Sophia will help you -- but only if you do it properly.' Toque uma vez, sem texto.
teacher.listening1_comp: Comprehension falada (3 min): Respostas em voz alta antes do clique. Depois: 'Why is the rule -- tell her first -- so important?' Deixe ele deduzir falando.
teacher.artefact: Artefato (3 min): Nao leia por ele. 'Read the escalation note, then escalate to me out loud, in five moves.' Ele fala; voce marca o que faltou.
teacher.grammar: Grammar discovery (4 min): Toque as 4 frases. Pergunta unica: 'Which one proves you tried? Which one shows the cost? Which one asks for something real?' Guie: I have asked = provas; if we do not... by = custo; I would like you to = pedido preciso.
teacher.rule: Reveal the Rule (3 min): Clique. Tentativas, custo, pedido. Exija 3 frases dele no present perfect sobre um bloqueio real. CCQ: 'I am waiting since two weeks -- correct? (No: I have been waiting FOR two weeks.)'
teacher.mistake: Common Mistake (2 min): 'since two weeks' e 'did not answered'. Peca as versoes certas em voz alta, duas vezes.
teacher.bank: Useful language falado (4 min): Cada frase lida em voz alta e imediatamente reescrita para o bloqueio REAL dele. Seis frases da tela, seis frases dele.
teacher.ch5: Transicao (1 min): 'Your boss will test you before she helps you.' Suba a tensao.
teacher.dialogue: Dialogo (6 min): Voce e a Sophia e voce TESTA: 'what have you tried?', 'give me a number, not a feeling', 'do not say help'. Antes de cada fala do Diogo, ELE fala primeiro; so entao clique e toque o audio.
teacher.dialogue_comp: Dialogue comprehension (2 min): Perguntas sobre a SOPHIA. Depois: 'Why does she ask about the relationship, and not only about the page?' Deixe ele falar.
teacher.listening2: Listening 2 (4 min): 'This is the escalation, done well -- 60 seconds.' Toque uma vez. Depois: 'What are the three attempts? What is the exact ask?' -- ele fala.
teacher.scenarios: Scenarios falados (5 min): Tres situacoes, 60 segundos cada, sem preparo: tentativas, custo, pedido preciso, e quem e avisado primeiro. Nao corrija no meio.
teacher.ch6: Transicao (1 min): 'From here you escalate, and my first answer is no. Three times.'
teacher.quickfire: Quick Fire (5 min): Uma situacao por vez, resposta imediata. Sem correcao entre elas -- fluencia primeiro.
teacher.rp_guided: Role-play GUIADO (4 min): Voce e a Sophia. Ele escala usando os cinco movimentos da nota. Se ele pedir 'help' em vez de algo preciso, mande refazer.
teacher.rp_semi: Role-play SEMI-LIVRE (5 min): Voce recusa duas vezes: 'sort it out yourself' e depois 'how do you work with her next month?'. Ele nao pode culpar a pessoa nem se emocionar: prova as tentativas, da o numero e diminui o pedido ate o NAO ficar caro.
teacher.rp_free: Role-play LIVRE (5 min): Sem apoio. 'A real blocker. Two minutes. One precise ask. Go.' NAO interrompa, cronometre e diga o tempo no fim.
teacher.pressure: Under pressure (4 min): Tres perguntas duras, uma a uma, sem aviso. Se ele atacar a pessoa em vez do problema, pare imediatamente e mande refazer a frase.
teacher.survival: Survival Card (3 min): 'Five phrases. With these you can escalate anything, and still have a colleague on Monday.' Toque cada uma; tom calmo e factual.
teacher.learned: Wrap-up (3 min): Ele clica cada check e diz a frase que prova. So depois devolva as 2 correcoes. Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos escalando um bloqueio real (tentativas + custo + pedido preciso).
teacher.closing: Fechamento (1 min): Homework ORAL: gravar 2 minutos de escalonamento e enviar. Anuncie a Aula 20: 'Next time is the final exam of this block: the steering committee, and they are looking for a mistake.'
